title: Windows update-bundle delta is bloated (391 MB) vs mac/linux (<2 MB) — likely CRLF/line-ending hashing
description: On the 0.0.11 -> 0.0.12 build (mpi-ci run #23), the per-platform DELTA update bundles came out: macOS 1.3 MB (14 files), Linux 0.8 MB (12 files), but WINDOWS 391 MB — a near-FULL bundle, not a delta. The actual 0.0.11->0.0.12 source change is ~12-14 files (launcher fix + version files), so mac/linux are correct and windows is wrong. The 391 MB windows update applied correctly during the online-update test (so it is a SIZE/bandwidth bug, not a correctness bug), but it means every windows delta ships near-full until fixed. LIKELY CAUSE: the windows release-baseline manifest (release-baselines/windows-x64.json, the 0.0.11 FULL portable-stage manifest committed 2026-06-10) has different file SHA256s than the 0.0.12 windows build for the SAME files — so the delta diff flags nearly every file as 'changed'. Most probable: CRLF vs LF line-ending normalization differing between the baseline build and this build (git autocrlf / .gitattributes interacting with how text files are staged on the windows CI runner), or a path-separator / file-mode difference in the hash input. INVESTIGATE: (1) diff a few unchanged text-file hashes between release-baselines/windows-x64.json and the 0.0.12 windows build's update-manifest.json — confirm they differ for files that did NOT change; (2) determine whether the windows CI runner stages text files with CRLF while the baseline was captured with LF (or vice versa); (3) check build-portable.mjs buildFileEntries/sha256 hashing — it hashes raw bytes, so any line-ending rewrite between builds changes the hash. FIX DIRECTION: make the staged file bytes deterministic across builds (respect .gitattributes eol on the windows runner so text files hash identically build-to-build), OR capture baselines on the same runner/normalization as the deltas. Found during the 0.0.12 online-update test (2026-06-10). NOT a correctness blocker for 1.0.0 (the bundle applies), but fix before relying on windows deltas for bandwidth.
